creator: knu@idaemons.org
text:
At Tue, 04 Sep 2001 17:13:45 +0200,
sheldon wrote:
> 	Frankly, I'm surprised the first CDROM doesn't provide Postfix,
> 	qmail, apache and other server-grade software, as used by ISP's
> 	and others in the server environment.

I suppose they should be added prior to (or along with) exim then.
Probably ProFTPD too. (Oh, procmail is not included either..)

Now, anybody know how much amount of space is left?  Can we add that
many extra packages?

> +CDROM_SET_1="${CDROM_SET_1} mail/exim"

> +CDROM_SET_1="${CDROM_SET_1} mail/exim-ldap"
> +CDROM_SET_1="${CDROM_SET_1} mail/exim-ldap2"
> +CDROM_SET_1="${CDROM_SET_1} mail/exim-monitor"
> +CDROM_SET_1="${CDROM_SET_1} mail/exim-mysql"
> +CDROM_SET_1="${CDROM_SET_1} mail/exim-postgresql"

Aren't these excessive?  OpenLDAP, MySQL and PostgreSQL are not
included in the CD-ROM #1 list currently.  They sound much like
optional.
